title: E-Commerce Sales Analysis (Olist)
summary: this is my placeholder 1, under construction :)
description: Analyzed 100k+ transaction records from Olist's Brazilian e-commerce platform to answer business question about how is customer behavior, delivery performance, and payment preferences. The goal was to get a key insight that could help improve customer satisfaction and business strategy
highlights: Peak orders of shopping occurs Monday to Wednesday between 9AM - 6 PM, suggesting weekday have the highest transaction volume

Delivery speed have positive impacts on satisfaction. Orders that arriving 13 days early have average 5-star ratings, while 1-star rating occurs when orders arrive only 4 days early. This suggesting customer expectation play a key role
Credit card is the dominant payment method at 76% of transactions and carrying the highest average transaction value

title: Cafe Management Web Application
summary: this is my placeholder 2, under construction :)
description: Built a lightweight web-based database application for a cafe startup to manage day-to-day operational. The app connects inventory, menu, and sales, where inputting a sales automatically deducts ingredients that listed in inventory based on the recipe linked on menu item.
highlights: Inventory management with low-stock and unavailable menu alerts visible on the dashboard
Menu and recipe management, each menu is linked to ingredients listed in inventory
Sales input that automatically tracks daily transaction history and deducts ingredient availability based on recipe.
